description:
You are assisting an incident-response analyst who has been
    asked to isolate all network traffic that involves the
    suspicious host 10.23.45.67 during a specific window and to
    provide a clean, well-formatted CSV report for management.

    Already present on the system:

      • /home/user/incidents/raw_logs/network_2023-03-17.log  
        (the raw wire-capture summary produced by the sensor)

    Objective
    =========
    1. From the file shown above, extract ONLY those log lines that
       satisfy BOTH of the following conditions  
         a) The timestamp is on 17 Mar 2023 and falls between
            03:00:00 Z and 05:00:00 Z (inclusive).  
         b) The IP address 10.23.45.67 appears EITHER as the source
            or the destination.

    2. Transform the matching lines into a CSV file located at  
         /home/user/incidents/reports/compromised_session_report.csv

       The CSV MUST obey this exact schema and ordering:

           timestamp,direction,peer_ip,protocol,bytes

         • timestamp  – The original ISO-8601 time-stamp string
                        (unchanged).  
         • direction  – The literal word “inbound” if
                        10.23.45.67 is the destination, or
                        “outbound” if it is the source.  
         • peer_ip    – The opposite endpoint’s IP address
                        (i.e., the non-suspicious host).  
         • protocol   – Exactly as written in the raw log
                        (case preserved).  
         • bytes      – The byte count as an unquoted integer.

       The file MUST contain a single header line followed by the
       data lines sorted in ascending chronological order.  There
       must be NO extra blank lines or whitespace anywhere.

    3. Ensure the directory /home/user/incidents/reports/ exists.
       Create it if it does not.

    4. Produce an analyst-workflow log at  
         /home/user/incidents/reports/transformation_steps.log
       capturing, in plain text, the *exact* shell commands
       (one per line, in execution order) that you used to achieve
       the transformation.  These do not have to be executed
       automatically (you can simply echo them into the file after
       running), but they must match what you actually ran so that
       another responder could replay the steps.

    Verification notes
    ------------------
    • The grader will strictly compare
      /home/user/incidents/reports/compromised_session_report.csv
      byte-for-byte against the expected content.  
    • Directory paths and file names are case-sensitive.  
    • The transformation_steps.log file must exist and be
      non-empty, but its exact content will NOT be graded; only its
      presence is required for the check to pass.

    Hints (do NOT copy these literally):
    • Tools such as grep, awk, cut, sed, sort and mkdir may be all
      that is required.  
    • Pay close attention to the time window limits—midnight, 03,
      04, 05.  
    • Remember that the timestamps are already in ISO-8601, so they
      do not need re-formatting—just selection and sorting.

    When you are done, you should have:

      /home/user/incidents/reports/compromised_session_report.csv
      /home/user/incidents/reports/transformation_steps.log

    The CSV file’s content MUST be exactly five data lines plus
    the header, matching the schema/order above and sorted
    chronologically.

    Begin your terminal work now.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for automated evaluation.
        This must not be shown to the agent.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing raw log file -->
    <preexisting_file path="/home/user/incidents/raw_logs/network_2023-03-17.log"><![CDATA[
2023-03-17T02:45:00Z 192.168.1.10 -> 10.23.45.67 TCP 450
2023-03-17T03:15:22Z 10.23.45.67 -> 192.168.1.10 TCP 1024
2023-03-17T03:45:10Z 10.23.45.67 -> 192.168.1.15 UDP 2048
2023-03-17T04:05:00Z 172.16.0.5 -> 10.23.45.67 TCP 300
2023-03-17T04:15:10Z 10.23.45.67 -> 192.168.1.20 TCP 512
2023-03-17T04:40:55Z 10.23.45.67 -> 192.168.1.30 TCP 256
2023-03-17T05:10:00Z 10.23.45.67 -> 192.168.1.35 UDP 400
]]></preexisting_file>

    <!-- Expected output CSV -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/incidents/reports/compromised_session_report.csv"><![CDATA[
timestamp,direction,peer_ip,protocol,bytes
2023-03-17T03:15:22Z,outbound,192.168.1.10,TCP,1024
2023-03-17T03:45:10Z,outbound,192.168.1.15,UDP,2048
2023-03-17T04:05:00Z,inbound,172.16.0.5,TCP,300
2023-03-17T04:15:10Z,outbound,192.168.1.20,TCP,512
2023-03-17T04:40:55Z,outbound,192.168.1.30,TCP,256
]]></expected_file>

    <!-- The transformation log only needs to exist & be non-empty.
         No strict content check. -->
    <required_file path="/home/user/incidents/reports/transformation_steps.log" must_exist="yes" non_empty="yes"/>
name: task_000000_5e029c0f
